seo.title: DiscVault. Built for collectors.
seo.description: Use your camera to scan barcodes, then automatically pull movie metadata and cover art. The app runs completely on its own — no server required. Manage your collection right on your device without the DiscVault backend.

hero.title: DiscVault 26 is here.
hero.lead: Built for people with real shelves, real stacks, and real editions.

routes.heading: Start self-hosted. Add mobile when it fits.
routes.mobileRoadmap: The iOS and Android apps work standalone today. Connecting them to a self-hosted DiscVault installation is planned for a later stage.

ios.label: iPhone & iPad
ios.title: Works standalone
ios.body: The app runs completely on its own — no server required. Manage your collection right on your device without the DiscVault backend.
points: Use your camera to scan barcodes, then automatically pull movie metadata and cover art.
Search by title, genre, or director and filter by format in a clean collection view.

selfHosted.label: Self-hosted
selfHosted.title: DiscVault 26 is here.
selfHosted.body: DiscVault 26 can be installed and used today. It brings a new desktop-first library, richer detail pages, PostgreSQL foundations, plugins, migration tools, and better admin controls for collectors who want to keep their physical media catalog self-hosted.
points: DiscVault 26 adds invite-only flows, owner/admin tools, Basic and Advanced RBAC, role simulation, audit views, API access, and MCP-ready integrations.
Manage metadata sources, receivers, import sources, digital media sources, plugin packages, health checks, and background plugin jobs from the admin area.

capabilities.heading: Built for people with real shelves, real stacks, and real editions.

items.title: Scan and enrich
items.body: Use your camera to scan barcodes, then automatically pull movie metadata and cover art.

items.title: Filter and search
items.body: Search by title, genre, or director and filter by format in a clean collection view.

items.title: New library experience
items.body: Browse posters, switch to list or detail views, filter by format, jump into containers and box sets, and keep the interface fast on large collections.

items.title: Richer movie and container pages
items.body: Detail pages now make room for backdrops, posters, videos, metadata, collection membership, watched state, and box-set contents.

items.title: Plugin-centered platform
items.body: Manage metadata sources, receivers, import sources, digital media sources, plugin packages, health checks, and background plugin jobs from the admin area.

items.title: Admin, RBAC, API and MCP
items.body: DiscVault 26 adds invite-only flows, owner/admin tools, Basic and Advanced RBAC, role simulation, audit views, API access, and MCP-ready integrations.

trust.heading: Works standalone
trust.body: The app runs completely on its own — no server required. Manage your collection right on your device without the DiscVault backend.
trust.noTrackingBody: No analytics, advertising, or third-party tracking.
trust.controlBody: Keep your library on your device or on a DiscVault server you administer.

comparison.heading: Built for people with real shelves, real stacks, and real editions.
comparison.iosBest: The app runs completely on its own — no server required. Manage your collection right on your device without the DiscVault backend.
comparison.iosSetup: Download from the App Store. No server required.
comparison.selfBest: DiscVault 26 can be installed and used today. It brings a new desktop-first library, richer detail pages, PostgreSQL foundations, plugins, migration tools, and better admin controls for collectors who want to keep their physical media catalog self-hosted.
comparison.selfSetup: Follow the current installation guide in DiscVault Docs.
comparison.androidSetup: Request access on Discord. Approved testers install through Google Play.

community.heading: Need help or want to follow updates?
